synonyms.note: 更常用，但不如 superior 正式
优秀的，但无比较含义

expressions.text: be allowed the freedom to do sth
expressions.usage: 写作中可用来表达“被给予做某事的自由”，例如：Students should be allowed the freedom to choose their own courses.
常用搭配，雅思写作中表达满意度时使用，例如：Customers are more satisfied with personalized services.

sentences.en: We don’t need to understand the intricate inner workings of AI systems, but if people are given a degree of responsibility for how they are implemented, they will be more willing to accept AI into their lives.
sentences.zh: 我们并不需要理解人工智能系统错综复杂的内部运作，但如果人们对这些系统如何被应用被赋予一定程度的责任，他们就会更愿意把人工智能接纳进自己的生活。

grammar.type: but 转折 + if 条件从句 + how 宾语从句
grammar.note: We don't need to understand... 为主句；but if people are given a degree of responsibility for... 为条件从句，含被动；how they are implemented 为 for 的宾语从句；主句 they will be more willing to accept AI；intricate 意为 “错综复杂的”，implement 意为 “实施、应用”。

pattern.label: S + Vt + O(主语 + 及物动词 + 宾语)

skeleton.text: the intricate inner workings of AI systems
skeleton.zh: AI系统错综复杂的内部运作

pattern.plain: 去掉修饰和从句后，第一分句只剩：We don't need to understand the workings (我们不需要理解运作)。第二分句主干：they will be more willing to accept AI (他们会更愿意接受AI)。

chunks.note: 第一分句的主语。
need to 是情态动词短语，don't 否定，整体作谓语。

chunks.text: the intricate inner workings of AI systems
chunks.zh: AI系统错综复杂的内部运作
chunks.note: understand 的宾语，名词词组。
逗号连接两个并列分句。
并列连词，表示转折。

chunks.text: if people are given a degree of responsibility for how they are implemented
chunks.zh: 如果人们对它们如何被实施被赋予一定程度的责任
chunks.note: 条件副词从句，修饰主句。
从句后逗号。
第二分句的主语，指代 people。

chunks.text: will be more willing to accept
chunks.note: will be willing to 是谓语，more 是比较级。
accept 的宾语。
介词短语作状语，修饰 accept。

grammar_points.name: 合句(并列句)
grammar_points.explain: 这句话由 but 连接两个完整的分句，形成并列句。第一个分句说“我们不需要理解...”，第二个分句说“但是...他们会更愿意接受”。but 表示前后意思相反或转折。

grammar_points.name: 条件副词从句
grammar_points.explain: if 引导的从句表示“如果...”，说明主句发生的条件。这里 if people are given... 是条件，主句 they will be more willing... 是结果。注意从句用了被动语态(are given)，因为人们是被赋予责任。

grammar_points.name: 名词从句
grammar_points.explain: how they are implemented 是一个名词从句，作介词 for 的宾语。how 引导的从句相当于一个名词，意思是“它们如何被实施”。这种结构在雅思阅读中很常见，用来表达“关于...的事情”。

aids.derivation: intric(纠缠)+-ate(形容词)→ 纠缠在一起的→错综复杂的
aids.mnemonic: in(里面)+tric( trick 把戏)→里面有很多把戏→错综复杂

synonyms.note: 更常用，雅思阅读中常替换 intricate
强调难以理解，也常见

aids.derivation: im(向内)+ple(填充)+ment(动词)→ 填充进去→实施
aids.mnemonic: im(我)+ple(请)+ment(门徒)→我请门徒来实施计划

synonyms.note: 更通用，雅思中常替换 implement
短语动词，口语和写作都常用

expressions.usage: 写作中可用来表达“某种程度的...”，如 a degree of freedom (一定程度的自由)。

questions.title: Questions 27–32
instructions: Reading Passage 3 has six sections, A–F.
Choose the correct heading for each section from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–viii, in boxes 27–32 on your answer sheet.
i An increasing divergence of attitudes towards AI
ii Reasons why we have more faith in human judgement than in AI
iii The superiority of AI projections over those made by humans
iv The process by which AI can help us make good decisions
v The advantages of involving users in AI processes
vi Widespread distrust of an AI innovation
vii Encouraging openness about how AI functions
viii A surprisingly successful AI application

pairs.p: Many decisions in our lives require a good forecast, and AI is almost always better at forecasting than we are.
pairs.note: 题干是段落标题，原文句是段落首句，直接对应段落内容。

paraphrase.explain: Section A 是段落标题，直接定位到原文段落首句，理解句意后匹配标题 iii。

pairs.p: Their attempt to promote this program to cancer doctors was a PR disaster.
pairs.note: 题干是段落编号，直接对应原文段落位置，无需替换。

paraphrase.explain: 先看题干是段落编号Section B，直接到原文找B段，定位句是段内关键句，匹配标题vi（可能对应‘公关灾难’或‘推广失败’）。

pairs.p: Trust in other people is often based on our understanding of how others think and having experience of their reliability.
pairs.note: 题干是章节编号，原文是段落内容，需通过匹配标题含义定位。

paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干Section C，到原文找对应段落，再根据段落主旨匹配标题ii。

pairs.p: society split
pairs.note: 题干中的'society split'与原文中的'society split'完全一致，是定位关键。

pairs.q: those who benefit from AI and those who reject it
pairs.p: those who benefit from AI and those who reject it
pairs.note: 题干中的描述与原文完全一致，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干中的'society split'和'those who benefit from AI and those who reject it'，到原文中找相同表述即可定位。

pairs.p: solution
pairs.note: 题干中的'solution'在原文中原词复现，是定位的关键词。

pairs.p: reveal more about
pairs.note: 题干中的'reveal more about'在原文中原词复现，帮助定位。

paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干中的'solution'和'reveal more about'，到原文中找到相同词汇即可定位到答案句。

pairs.p: Research suggests that allowing people some control over AI decision-making could also improve trust and enable AI to learn from human experience.
pairs.note: 题干是段落编号，定位句是F段原文，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: Section F是段落编号，直接到原文找F段，定位句就是该段内容。

questions.title: Questions 33–35
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 33–35 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
What is the writer doing in Section A?
A providing a solution to a concern
B justifying an opinion about an issue
C highlighting the existence of a problem
D explaining the reasons for a phenomenon

pairs.p: Yet for all these technological advances, we still seem to deeply lack confidence in AI predictions.
pairs.note: 题干问Section A的内容，定位句直接来自Section A，属于原词复现。

pairs.q: highlighting the existence of a problem
pairs.p: lack confidence in AI predictions
pairs.note: 'lack confidence'对应'problem'，即缺乏信心是一个问题。

paraphrase.trap: 选项A（提供解决方案）或D（解释原因）可能被误选，但原文只指出问题，未提方案或原因。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干'Section A'和'problem'，到原文找Section A中表示问题的表述，发现'lack confidence'即问题所在。

items.prompt:
According to Section C, why might some people be reluctant to accept AI?
A They are afraid it will replace humans in decision-making jobs.
B Its complexity makes them feel that they are at a disadvantage.
C They would rather wait for the technology to be tested over a period of time.
D Misunderstandings about how it works make it seem more challenging than it is.

pairs.p: cause anxiety and give us a sense that we’re losing control
pairs.note: 题干说‘不愿接受AI’，原文说‘引发焦虑和失控感’，这是原因和结果的替换。

pairs.q: complexity makes them feel at a disadvantage
pairs.p: interacting with something we don’t understand
pairs.note: 题干‘复杂性让他们处于劣势’对应原文‘与不理解的东西打交道’，‘不理解’就是复杂性的体现。

pairs.p: anxiety
pairs.note: ‘不愿’和‘焦虑’是情感上的同义替换。

paraphrase.trap: 选项A‘害怕AI取代人类决策工作’和选项D‘误解使其看起来更难’在原文未提及，选项C‘等待技术测试’也不对应。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘reluctant’和‘complexity’，到原文找‘anxiety’和‘don’t understand’，定位到答案句。

items.prompt:
What does the writer say about the media in Section C of the text?
A It leads the public to be mistrustful of AI.
B It devotes an excessive amount of attention to AI.
C Its reports of incidents involving AI are often inaccurate.
D It gives the impression that AI failures are due to designer error.

pairs.p: media
pairs.note: 题干和原文都出现了'media'，是定位的锚点词。

pairs.q: leads the public to be mistrustful of AI
pairs.p: emphasising the message that we cannot rely on technology
pairs.note: 题干说'导致公众不信任AI'，原文说'强调我们不能依赖技术的信息'，意思一致。

pairs.p: disproportionate amount of media attention
pairs.note: 'disproportionate'（不成比例的）与'excessive'（过度的）是同义替换。

paraphrase.trap: 选项B说媒体过度关注AI，但原文说的是'AI failures'（AI失败）而非AI本身，所以B偷换了概念。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'media'和'mistrustful of AI'，到原文找'media'和'cannot rely on technology'，发现原文说AI失败得到过多媒体关注，强化了不信任，对应选项A。

questions.title: Questions 36–40
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 36–40 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: Subjective depictions of AI in sci-fi films make people change their opinions about automation.

pairs.q: make people change their opinions about automation
pairs.p: polarised the participants’ attitudes
pairs.note: 题干说‘改变看法’，原文说‘使态度两极分化’，意思相近但方向不同：原文强调分化而非单纯改变。

pairs.q: Subjective depictions of AI in sci-fi films
pairs.p: regardless of whether the film they watched depicted AI in a positive or negative light
pairs.note: 题干‘主观描绘’对应原文‘无论正面或负面描绘’，但原文强调‘无论何种描绘’，而题干隐含主观性。

paraphrase.trap: 题干说‘改变看法’，但原文说‘两极分化’，即有人变得更积极、有人变得更消极，而非所有人改变看法。且原文未提及‘自动化’(automation)，而是‘技术未来’。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘change opinions’和‘automation’，到原文找对应。原文说‘polarised attitudes’（两极分化）而非‘change’（改变），且未提‘automation’，因此判断为NO。

items.prompt: Portrayals of AI in media and entertainment are likely to become more positive.

pairs.q: Portrayals of AI in media and entertainment
pairs.p: AI is represented more and more in media and entertainment
pairs.note: 题干中的'Portrayals'（描绘）替换为原文的'represented'（呈现），'in media and entertainment'原词复现。

pairs.q: are likely to become more positive
pairs.p: could lead to a society split
pairs.note: 题干说'可能变得更积极'，原文说'可能导致社会分裂'，意思相反，但原文未提及'积极'，因此无对应。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中的'positive'在原文中没有对应词，原文讨论的是社会分裂，而非积极或消极，因此是NOT GIVEN。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'Portrayals of AI in media and entertainment'和'positive'。到原文找到'AI is represented more and more in media and entertainment'，但未提及'positive'，且原文说的是'lead to a society split'，与'positive'无关，因此答案NOT GIVEN。

items.prompt: Rejection of the possibilities of AI may have a negative effect on many people’s lives.

pairs.q: Rejection of the possibilities of AI
pairs.p: refusing to accept the advantages offered by AI
pairs.note: 题干中的‘拒绝AI的可能性’对应原文的‘拒绝接受AI带来的好处’，是同义替换。

pairs.q: negative effect on many people’s lives
pairs.p: place a large group of people at a serious disadvantage
pairs.note: 题干中的‘对许多人的生活产生负面影响’对应原文的‘使一大群人处于严重不利地位’，是同义替换。

paraphrase.trap: 注意题干中的‘negative effect’可能被误解为‘坏处’，但原文明确说‘拒绝接受好处’会导致不利，因此是肯定态度。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘Rejection’和‘negative effect’，到原文找‘refusing’和‘disadvantage’，发现意思一致，因此答案为YES。

items.prompt: Familiarity with AI has very little impact on people’s attitudes to the technology.

pairs.p: having previous experience with AI
pairs.note: 'Familiarity'（熟悉）替换为'previous experience'（以往经验），意思相同。

pairs.p: significantly improve
pairs.note: 题干说'影响很小'，原文说'显著改善'，意思相反，所以答案为NO。

pairs.p: people’s opinions
pairs.note: 'attitudes'（态度）替换为'opinions'（看法），同义词。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中的'very little impact'容易让人误以为与原文的'significantly improve'是正向替换，实际是反向。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'Familiarity with AI'和'very little impact'，到原文找'previous experience with AI'和'significantly improve'，发现意思相反，所以选NO。

items.prompt: AI applications which users are able to modify are more likely to gain consumer approval.

pairs.q: AI applications which users are able to modify
pairs.p: allowed the freedom to slightly modify an algorithm
pairs.note: 题干说'用户能够修改的AI应用'，原文说'被允许自由地稍作修改算法'，意思相同，但表达方式不同。

pairs.q: more likely to gain consumer approval
pairs.p: more likely to use it in the future
pairs.note: 题干说'更可能获得消费者认可'，原文说'更可能在未来使用它'，使用是认可的一种表现。

paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'modify'和'gain consumer approval'，到原文找对应表达，发现'allowed the freedom to slightly modify'和'more likely to use it in the future'，意思一致，因此选YES。
